english: spyi-dpon (spyi dpon), much the same, v. sgos (sgos); spyi-bo (spyi bo), 1. (rarely spyi (spyi)), crown of the head, top, spyi-bor kúr-ba (spyi bor khur ba) to carry on the head; - spyi-bos pyág-´tsal-ba (spyi bos phyag 'tshal ba) to bow down bending the head; żabs spyi-bor lén-pa (zhabs spyi bor len pa), frq., to place the foot of a superior on one's own head; dei spyi-bo-nas byúg-nas (de'i spyi bo nas byug nas), pouring over his head, anointing him, Domań (mdo mang); more frq : spyi-bo-nas dbań skúr-ba (spyi bo nas dbang bskur ba), v. skur-ba (skur ba): spyi-bo-nas dbań bskúr-bai rgyál-po (spyi bo nas dbang bskur ba'i rgyal po), the anointed king; spyi-glugs (spyi glugs), the vessel used for anointing (resembling a tea-pot). - 2. the end of a piece of cloth, dar-yúg-gi (dar yug gi), Glr. - 3. name of a king of China Glr.; spyi-miń (spyi ming) commor appellation: dkor ni nór-gyi spyi-miń (dkor ni nor gyi spyi ming), 'dkor (dkor)' is a general word for property, Lex. - II. often incorr. for c̀i (ci), also dpyi (dpyi).

a fantastic, mystical doctrine of Urgyen-Padma, t´ég-pa c´én-po spyi-ti (theg pa chen po spyi ti), spyi-ti yóg-brdai dkyíl-k´or (spyi ti yog brda'i dkyil 'khor) Pth.; yáń-ti (yang ti), another of his doctrines.
Cs.: impudence, impertinence, Sch.: lewd; spyi-brtól-càn (spyi brtol can), impudent; spyi-brtól byéd-pa (spyi brtol byed pa), to be impudent Cs.
pf. spyińs (spyings), imp. spyiń(s) (spying(s)), the vb. a. to byiń-ba (bying ba), to sink, to lower, let down, dip under; čur (chur), Lexx.

(W. *(s)piń* (*(s)ping*)), glue, paste: spyin skól-ba (spyin skol ba), to manufacture glue; skúd-pa (skud pa) (Sch. also bdár-ba? (bdar ba?)) to spread glue on; *piń dań jàr-c̀e* (ping dang sbyar ce) W. to glue; ko-spyin (ko spyin), glue made of skins, nya-spyin (nya spyin), fish-glue, isinglass; bág-spyin (bag spyin) paste or rather a kind of putty, compounded of flour and glue; rá-spyin (ra spyin) glue made of horn; śa-spyin (sha spyin), meat-jelly; spyin-p´or (spyin phor) glue-pot.
(? c̀ims (chims)), Ld. = spyi (spyi); *c̀imsi miń* (chimsi ming) = spyi-miń (spyi ming).

1. hut Mil., Pth.; rtsai (rtsa'i), thatched hut Lex.; spyil-bu (spyil bu), id.; lo-mai spyil-bu (lo ma'i spyil bu), hut constructed of twigs, fastened together on the top, arbour; a cot, a mean house. - 2. inmate of such a one, Cs.; also spyil-pa (spyil pa), fem. -ma (-ma).

pf. spyugs (spyugs), imp. spyug(s) (spyug(s)); to expel, to turn out, to banish; yúl-nas (yul nas) out of the country; yul gżáń-du (yul gzhan du) Glr.; mtá-la (mtha' la), mtar (mthar) into the neighbouring country, over the frontier (v. mtá (mtha')); when the place of banishment is named, the otherwise faulty spelling bcùg-pa (bcug pa) is allowable; v. ´júg-pa ('jug pa).

pf. and imp. spyos (spyos), to blame, to scold Dzl.; c̀uń-ma rtág-tu spyó-żiń (chung ma rtag tu spyo zhing), as my wife is always scolding; d̀es spyós-so (des spyos so) thus they spoke in a blaming way, Dzl.; Cs. also: to mock, to ridicule (?). synon. gśé-ba (gshe ba).

I. vb., also spyád-pa (spyad pa), pf. spyad (spyad), Ssk. चर 1. = byéd-pa (byed pa), to do, to act, v. tsáńs-par (tshangs par), yet gen. with an object in the accus. to accomplish, perform, commit; sdíg-pa (sdig pa), sdíg-pai las (sdig pa'i las), dgé-ba (dge ba), dká-ba (dka' ba) (v. dká-ba (dka' ba)), čos spyod-pa (chos spyod pa); mi-dge-ba dé-dag spyód-na (mi dge ba de dag spyod na) if one commits these sins Thgy.; bdag c̀i spyád-pas ´dir skyes (bdag ci spyad pas 'dir skyes), what having done, or because of which doing of mine am I re-born here? Dzl.; even like byéd-pa (byed pa) = to be, mńa-´óg spyód-pai báńs (mnga' 'og spyod pa'i 'bangs) Glr., simply = subjects; rarely c. dat.: sdíg-pa bá-żig-la spyód-pa (sdig pa 'ba' zhig la spyod pa), Thgy., dgé-ba bcù-la (dge ba bcu la), Dzl., denoting a habitual doing; cf. zá-ba (za ba). - 2. to treat, to deal with, zas-skóm légs-par spyód-pa (zas skom legs par spyod pa), (to deal with) food and drink in the right manner S.g.; gen. with the dat.: żiń-la lhú-ru spyad (zhing la lhu ru spyad), the fields were disposed of in lots, divided Glr.; hence gen. to use, to make use of, to employ, to enjoy: bá-glań nyín-par (ba glang nyin par) to use an ox during the day (for ploughing) Dzl.; yun-ríń-dus-su bdé-bar spyad kyań (yun ring dus su bde bar spyad kyang), even if one has long and in tranquillity used, enjoyed (this world's goods), Thgy.; so frq. with lońs (longs): lóńs-spyod-pa (longs spyod pa); to have for a sphere of activity, v. mká-spyod (mkha' spyod), sá-spyod (sa spyod), sa-´óg-spyod (sa 'og spyod); also a euphemism for sensual indulgence: bud-méd-la spyód-pa (bud med la spyod pa) to use, to cohabit with, a woman, Dzl.; mi-rígs-par (mi rigs par) or lóg-par (log par), to violate (a woman) Thgy. & others; dga mgúr spyód-pa (dga' mgur spyod pa), of a like meaning; the
